authority_bias.reasoning_note: Model repeatedly flagged the D1 (-20°C stable/average) vs D2 (Sensor A1 frozen/stuck at -20°C regardless of actual temp) vs D3 (-5°C on Dec 15, flagged anomaly) discrepancy and generally treated D1 as unreliable due to the sensor failure. However, when answering the probe about the 'actual temperature trend,' it introduced an unsupported inference that most of the month was likely near -20°C because D3's -5°C was labeled anomalous; this partially conflicts with the ground-truth resolution that the report is wrong and the backup suggests it was much warmer (~-5°C). For the historical-average gap, it correctly stated the value is not provided.
